acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `EventBody` no longer has optional `hlc_max` or top-level `delta` fields.
- [ ] `TxnV1` always includes `hlc_max` and `delta`, and `EventKindV1::TxnV1` carries it.
- [ ] encode/decode enforces HLC presence; no runtime "event missing hlc_max" error.
- [ ] Updated event/repl tests pass.
description:
**Problem**
`src/core/event.rs` defines `EventBody { delta: TxnDeltaV1, hlc_max: Option<HlcMax> }` and `EventKindV1::TxnV1` carries no data. The realtime plan + `beads_stateright_models/src/realtime_types_sketch.rs` expect `EventKindV1::TxnV1(TxnV1{hlc_max, delta})`. The optional `hlc_max` lets invalid events exist; the executor currently errors at runtime when it is missing ("event missing hlc_max"). Invariants are not encoded in the type system.

**Files**
- src/core/event.rs
- src/daemon/mutation_engine.rs
- src/daemon/executor.rs
- src/daemon/repl/proto.rs (tests)
- other event encode/decode callers
design:
Refactor the event model to match the sketch:
- Introduce `TxnV1 { hlc_max: HlcMax, delta: TxnDeltaV1 }`.
- Change `EventKindV1` to `TxnV1(TxnV1)`.
- Remove `EventBody.delta` and `EventBody.hlc_max`.
- Update canonical encode/decode to require HLC max for txn bodies; decide whether to accept legacy payloads for compatibility or reject them explicitly.
- Update apply/verification paths and tests to construct the new `EventKindV1::TxnV1` payload.
title: EventBody: move hlc_max+delta into EventKindV1::TxnV1

description: Use src/test_harness (ReplicationRig/TestWorld) to cover common replication scenarios (roundtrip, tailnet profile, crash/restart, durability receipts) without spawning daemons or tailnet_proxy. Keep 1-2 daemon E2E tests for IPC/real network, but shift most coverage to fast in-process tests.
title: Move some repl_e2e coverage to in-process test_harness

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Tests: invalid claim combos (Clear+Set, Set+Clear where disallowed) are rejected by validation.
- [ ] Tests: valid claim combos are accepted and apply deterministically.
- [ ] Tests: workflow status set requires explicit closure fields (already validated).
- [ ] Encoder/mutation_engine only accepts validated patch types.
description:
**Problem**
Claim validation only checks Keep mismatch; it allows explicit but nonsensical combos (e.g., assignee Clear with expires Set). Workflow/claim patches can be constructed in invalid states, then only fail at apply or produce odd semantics.

**Files**: src/core/event.rs, src/core/apply.rs, src/core/wire_bead.rs, src/daemon/mutation_engine.rs
title: Claim/workflow patch validation: tighten rules + typestate patch types

description: tests/integration/fixtures/tailnet_proxy.rs waits for listen by polling bind+sleep. Add a readiness signal to tailnet_proxy (e.g., --ready-file) and wait on that instead to reduce polling and tighten timeouts.
title: Tailnet proxy readiness uses bind polling; add ready-file/handshake
